observations.recommendation:
a) Reassess and reconsider the internal controls, policies and procedures adopted
by the municipality in recognizing, monitoring, safeguarding and reporting of its
PPE:
b) Intensify the institutionalization of an Inventory Committee to conduct actual
physical inventory of its PPE annually, taking into consideration the required
standards and procedures in planning, execution, and reconciliation of GSO and
Accounting records;
c) Instruct the Municipal Accountant to maintain and update the PPE ledger cards
and the GSO of its property cards as control records to facilitate the regular
reconciliation of records and conduct further investigation into the circumstances
surrounding the loss of some items that were not found during the actual count;
and
d) Direct the Municipal Accountant to provide adequate adjustments in the books of
accounts for the reclassification and derecognition of PPE accounts as well as the
additional provision for depreciation.

To the Municipal Accountant:
a) Prepare adjusting journal entry/ies to recognize all the valid reconciling items,
including the take up the unrecorded bank account and effect the necessary
entries on the restoration of cash of the stale check;
To the Municipal Treasurer:
b) Regularly monitor the status of checks released and unreleased. Send written
notice to the concerned payee regarding the existence of the checks at least a
month before it becomes stale, in compliance with Section 59 of Volume I of the
Manual on NGAS; and
c) Return the original DVs and supporting documents of the staled checks to the
Municipal Accountant who will prepare a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to record
the transaction as Payable;
To Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer:
d) Perform a regular reconciliation of the accounts to avoid the accumulation of
unreconciled items in the books; and
e) Analyze the dormant bank account and communicate with the source agency to
determine the nature and terms of the account. If warranted, cause the closure of
the dormant account, and transfer the balance to the general fund to protect the
amount from possible risk of loss of rights and ownership.

In view of the foregoing observation, we recommended the Management to evaluate
the property tax receivable accounts and validate their existence and collectability.
Implement appropriate measures in compliance with IPSAS 41 and COA Circular No.
2023-008, particularly in recognizing an allowance for impairment losses, if
necessary, to ensure accurate financial reporting and prevent the overstatement of
receivables in the financial statements.

a) Maximize its authority and power to collect taxes, fees and other charges and
formulate a more effective and efficient collection procedures and strategies;
b) Revisit its revenue code and consider additional sources of income as authorized
under RA 7160 to increase revenues and lessen its dependence on the internal
revenue allotments; and
c) Strictly implement the tax ordinances enacted by the Sangguniang Bayan relative
to revenue generation program.

a) To the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to direct the concerned offices to expedite the
implementation of the in-progress and unimplemented 20% Local Development
Fund programs and projects to fully attain the desired socio-economic
development and environmental management outcomes essential to the
promotion of the general welfare; and
b) To the Local Development Council to identify the cause of the delays or non-
implementation of the PPAs and determine whether the projects are still relevant
and align with the local government unit's priorities and needs.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended that the Management maximize the
utilization of the LDRRM fund to effectively increase the level of preparedness of the
Municipality during natural calamities and identify the cause of the delays or non-
implementation of the PPAs and determine whether the projects are still relevant and
align with the local government unit's priorities and needs.

a) Require the Heads of implementing departments/offices to ensure that GAD
projects, activities and programs are implemented as planned to attain the GAD
objectives;
b) Maximize the utilization of the GAD funds through the implementation of GAD-
related programs and projects in order to attain the objective for which funds were
provided;
c) Require the GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) of the Municipality to have a
continuous education on GAD related activities; and
d) Require the GFPS to establish a more adequate, updated and systematically
gathered sex-disaggregated data which will provide the bases for gender analysis,
planning, programming and policy formulation and establish a GAD monitoring
and evaluation system to come up with a more GAD responsive and related
programs and activities.

We reiterated our recommendation that the management expedite the establishment
of the Septage and Sewage Management Ordinance (SSMO) and to collaborate with
relevant stakeholders in the locality to ensure its effective implementation within its
jurisdiction.

a. Coordinate with the Accounting Office to fast-track the reconciliation of the inventory reports and accounting records.
b. Continue and expedite the reconciliation process of the one-time cleansing of the PPE accounts.
c. Ensure that the proper accounting and reporting procedures for all acquisition/issuance/transfer/disposal of government assets are pursuant to the existing manuals and other relevant laws, rules and regulations after the completion of one-time cleansing of the PPE accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the agency head: a) Ensure the optimal utilization of the 20% EDF by prioritizing the projects and maximizing the 20% fund; b) Strengthen and/or adjust existing planning and budgeting practices and techniques to further improve the orderliness of the Municipality’s processes to be able to comply with the Synchronized Planning and Budgeting Calendar for LGUs; and c) Avoid and discontinue the practice of appropriating and charging expenses that are not allowed under the 20% Economic Development Fund and/or not connected with the implementation of development projects.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality is trying their best to maximize the utilization of the 20% EDF by identifying and prioritizing projects. The identified PPAs were already included in the reprogramming for CY 2024 which was endorsed to the Local Sangguniang Bayan on March 18, 2024. Management committed to call for prompt implementation of the priority projects and will ensure PPAs under the 20% DF for succeeding years must be well-planned and procurement-and-implementation ready.
observations.reason: There are some factors that causes non-implementation of projects like delay in the procurement process, or problems just before a project is started. Significant portion of unimplemented PPA for the current year appropriation was attributable to the fund intended for COVID-19 vaccination, which remained unutilized by year-end since COVID-19 infection rates, severity and vaccine turnover were very low during CY 2023.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the following to the Management: a) Ensure conduct of orderliness in quarterly assessment of LDRRMF to prudently monitor its utilization and address gaps in achieving the disaster-resilient community objectives; b) Give priority and properly implement disaster-related PPAs and optimize the utilization of its LDRRM Fund; c) Prudently monitor the unutilized prior year balances (within five (5) years) of the QRF and DRRMF MOOE to the 'Trust Liability DRRM' account, pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-12, and revert back to the unappropriated surplus of the General Fund any unutilized amount after five years; and d) Warrant the veracity and reliability of the prepared and submitted report in order to ensure appropriate decision-making.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality managed to utilize a substantial portion of the LDRRMF for CY 2023. Due to complexity of monitoring all LDRRMF transactions that occurred in CY 2023, added with the accounting for the reprogramming of the 2020-2021 unutilized balances, transferring of the unexpended balances of CY 2023 (QRF and MF-MOOE) to the Trust Liability-DRRMF account was not considered. An adjusting entry has already been made as of February 2024. Management will update their format in preparing the Notes to Financial Statements pertaining to the DRRM Fund in the ensuing years and will continue optimizing utilization and judicious monitoring in accordance with relevant laws and regulations.
observations.reason: There are still some gaps in recording and reporting due to the complexity of the reporting and accounting for LDRRMF and the reprogramming of prior year unutilized balances.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the agency head: a) Reassess and reconsider the internal controls, policies and procedures adopted in recognizing, monitoring, safeguarding and reporting of its Property, Plant and Equipment; b) Intensify the institutionalization of an Inventory Committee to conduct actual physical inventory of its PPE annually, establish the correctness, completeness and reliability of the PPE accounts; c) Instruct the Municipal Accountant to maintain and update the PPE ledger cards and submit the property cards as control records to facilitate regular reconciliation of the records and investigate any discrepancy noted; d) Ensure that all insurable physical assets are adequately and reliably protected against any damage to or loss of properties pursuant to Republic Act No. 656; e) Facilitate the preparation of the documentary requirements to secure the Transfer Certificates of Title (TCT) under the name of the Municipality of Matalam, Cotabato for all parcels of land to support claims of ownership and to avoid loss of properties; and f) Require the Municipal Accountant to recognize all completed Road Network Assets on cost or fair value whichever is applicable and direct concerned offices to strictly perform their duties and responsibilities in the accounting and reporting of Local Road Network as mandated under COA Circular No. 2015-008.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the agency head: a) Strictly adhere to Item V of COA Circular No. 2014-001 dated March 18, 2014 for the timely submission of reports to COA audit team; b) Require the Heads of implementing departments/offices to ensure that GAD projects, activities and programs are implemented as planned to attain the GAD objectives; c) Maximize the utilization of the GAD funds through the implementation of GAD-related programs and projects; d) Comply with the requirement of Section 37 (C), Rule VI of RA No. 9710 or the Magna Carta of Women and Section 4.1A of PCW- DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01; e) Require the GAD Focal Point System to institutionalize the GAD database with adequate and systematically gathered sex-disaggregated data and undertake gender analysis; and f) Establish a GAD monitoring and evaluation system as required under Sections 3.4 and 5.1 of PCW- DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01.

observations.recommendation:
In view of the foregoing observations, we recommended and Management agreed on
the following courses of action to be taken by the agency head, viz:

a) Instruct the Municipal Accountant to maintain and update the PPE ledger cards
and the GSO of its property cards as control records to facilitate the regular
reconciliation of records and conduct further investigation into the circumstances
surrounding the loss of some items that were not found during the actual count. If
still unlocated, determine the concerned personnel liable for the loss and require
them to pay for the cost of the property since the required request for
accountability has not been filed within the prescribed period;
b) Ensure that all insurable physical assets are adequately and reliably protected
against any damage to or loss of properties pursuant to Republic Act No. 656;
c) Give instruction to the Municipal Treasurer to fast-track the preparation of
documentary requirements to secure the Transfer Certificates of Title (TCT) under
the name of the Municipality of Matalam for all parcels of land to support claims of
ownership and provide copies to the audit team; and also trace
records/documents to establish ownership of the parcels of land with incomplete
data as to acquisition date and cost and if found to be legally owned by the
Municipality, expedite the application for the land titles under the name of the
Municipality of Matalam, Cotabato to avoid loss of properties in case other
interested parties may claim the same in the future;
d) Require the Municipal Accountant to recognize all completed Road Network
Assets on cost or fair value whichever is applicable and direct the Municipal
Accountant, the MGSO, the Municipal Engineer and the Inventory Committee to
strictly perform their duties and responsibilities in the accounting and reporting of
Local Road Network as mandated under COA Circular No. 2015-008 and submit
the pertinent reports as prescribed by existing laws, rules and regulations; and
e) Direct the Inventory Committee to strictly adhere and comply to the provisions of
COA Circular 2020-006 which provides for the Guidelines and Procedures in the
Conduct of Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (PPE), Recognition of
PPE Items Found at Station, and Disposition for Non-existing/Missing PPE Items,
for the One-Time Cleansing of PPE Account Balances of Government Agencies and
to conduct a complete inventory taking and submit the Report on Physical Count of
Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) every January 31 of each year to the
Office of the Auditor.

In view of the foregoing observations, we recommended the following courses of action:
To Municipal Accountant:
a. Prepare an adjusting journal entry to recognize all the valid reconciling items and
effect the necessary entries on the restoration of cash of the stale checks;
b. Furnish immediately the bank of the Bank Reconciliation Statement and confer with
the concerned bank personnel all the noted bank reconciling items.
To Municipal Treasurer:
c. Regularly monitor the status of checks released and unreleased. Send written
notice to the concerned payee regarding the existence of the checks at least a
month before it becomes stale in compliance with Section 59 of Volume I of the
Manual on NGAS;
d. Return the original DVs and supporting documents of the staled checks to the
Municipal Accountant who will prepare a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to record the
transaction as Accounts Payable; and
To Municipal Accountant and Treasurer:
e. Perform a regular reconciliation of the accounts to avoid the accumulation of
unreconciled items in the books.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended the following courses of actions;

a) To the Local Chief Executive to require the offices/officials concerned to identify
the gaps in the determination and implementation of development projects
thereby come up with corrective measures to ensure optimal utilization of funds
and timely delivery of value-added services and benefits to constituents;
b) To the Local Development Council and Local Finance Committee to assign
specific timelines for the identified development projects to evaluate achievement
rate and identify areas with major shortfall or needs improvement in order to make
timely and periodic remedial measures and determine the feasibility of
implementation of prior year’s unimplemented projects;
c) To the Municipal Engineering Office to submit status report of all the continuing
programs, projects and activities including the plans and action for the
pending/unimplemented programs at the beginning of ensuing year in accordance
with Section 2.1 of COA Circular 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013; and
d) To the Local Budget Officer to determine the status of excess funds from
completed projects and ensure that the aforementioned funds were free from any
encumbrances and recommend to the Local Chief Executive and Sanggunian
Bayan for its reversion and re-appropriate it to other development projects of the
LGU.

In view of the foregoing observations, we recommended and Management agreed on
the following:

a) Ascertain that those provisions in the Republic Act No. 10121 are properly
complied specifically the allotment for the LDRRM Fund which will improve the
capacity of the Municipality to have a well-defined and budgeted LDRRM Plan;
b) Ensure that allotted budget for the LDRRM Fund were properly appropriated for
the year to ascertain that all programs, projects and activities identified in the
LDRRM Fund will be realized accordingly and reported appropriately;
c) Properly implement the LDRRM Plan and optimize the utilization of its LDRRM
Fund to lessen the socioeconomic and environmental impacts of disasters in the
community and disaster risks, and to enhance disaster preparedness and
response capabilities of the community;
d) Include in the plans the utilization of the unexpended LDRRM Fund balances from
prior years, and specify the list of programs, projects, and activities to be charged
against the unexpended LDRRM Fund of previous years in its LDRRM Fund
Investment Plan before utilization of the same pursuant to the provisions of COA
Circular No. 2012-002; and
e) Record and monitor the unutilized prior year balances (within five (5) years) of the
QRF and DRRMF-MOOE to the “Trust Liability-DRRM” account pursuant to COA
Circular No. 2012-12. Accordingly, revert back to the unappropriated surplus of the
General Fund any unutilized amount after five years as mandated in the above-
mentioned Circular.

In view of the foregoing observations, we recommended and Management agreed on
the following:

a) Strictly adhere to Item V of COA Circular No. 2014-001 dated March 18, 2014 for
the timely submission of the said reports to the COA audit team;
b) Allocate at least 5% of the total annual budget for the implementation of GAD-
related activities as required under Section 36(a) of RA No. 9710 or the Magna
Carta of Women;
c) Require the Heads of implementing departments/offices to ensure that GAD
projects, activities and programs are implemented as planned to attain the GAD
objectives;
d) Maximize the utilization of the GAD funds through the implementation of GAD-
related programs and projects in order to attain the objective for which funds were
provided; and
e) Require the GAD Focal Point System to institutionalize the GAD database with
adequate, updated and systematically gathered sex-disaggregated data which will
provide the bases for gender analysis, planning, programming and policy
formulation and establish a GAD monitoring and evaluation system to come up
with a more GAD responsive and related programs and activities.

In view of the foregoing observations, we recommended and Management agreed on
the following:

a) Strictly adhere to the provision set forth in the DILG MC No. 2019-149 especially
on the prompt and updated posting of the required documents in at least three (3)
conspicuous places;
b) Capacitate the General Services Office in terms of manpower, trainings and other
resources to provide a more efficient and effective services;
c) Require the General Services Office to be prompt and accurate on the posting of
the required documents and instruct the Department Heads of concerned offices for
the timely submission of the reports; and
d) Maximize the utilization of the FDP Board and website of the Municipality.

We recommended that the agency head: a) Reassess and reconsider the internal controls, policies and procedures adopted by the Municipality in recognizing, monitoring, safeguarding and reporting of its Property, Plant and Equipment (PPEs) ledger card and property cards. b) Intensify the institutionalization of an Inventory Committee to conduct actual physical inventory annually, taking into consideration the required standards and procedures in Planning, Execution, and Reconciliation of GSO and Accounting records; c) Instruct the Municipal Accountant to maintain and update the PPE ledger cards and the GSO of its property cards as control records to facilitate the regular reconciliation of records and investigate any discrepancy noted; d) Ensure that all insurable physical assets are adequately and reliably protected against any damage to or loss of properties pursuant to Republic Act No. 656; e) Facilitate the preparation of documentary requirements to secure the Transfer Certificates of Title (TCT) under the name of the Municipality of Matalam, Cotabato for all parcels of land to support claims of ownership and to avoid loss of properties in case other interested parties may claim the same in the future; and trace its records/documents to establish ownership of the parcels of land with incomplete data as to acquisition date and cost and if found to be legally owned by the Municipality, expedite the application for the land titles under the name of the Municipality of Matalam, Cotabato; f) Require the Municipal Accountant to recognize all completed Road Network Assets on cost or fair value whichever is applicable and direct the Municipal Accountant, the MGSO, the Municipal Engineer and the Inventory Committee to strictly perform their duties and responsibilities in the accounting and reporting of Local Road Network as mandated under COA Circular No. 2015-008.
observations.managementAction: The Accounting Office is now in the process of reviewing, reconciling and updating the records of the Property, Plant & Equipment: Hence, this was reiterated and incorporated in Part II of this AAR. As of this writing, one of the noted Land Accounts has already been transferred under the name of the LGU. The management also allocated additional funds to finance the transfer of the other lands. observations.recommendation: a) The MPDC should always ensure that all expenses incurred are included on the Supplemental Investment Plan; b) Prepare and submit additional/updated supplemental investment plan for the programs and projects intended to locally stranded individuals and returning overseas individuals; c) Incorporate the updated investment plan to the supplemental budget and submit the said plans to COA. a) Require the Local Finance Committee to strictly monitor the funds intended for the programs and project; b) Require the respective office to implement PPAs according to the timeframes, approved budgets, and intended purposes; and c) Return any unutilized balance to the National Treasury after the lifting of the State of Calamity in accordance to Section 3.9 of DBM LBC No. 125 dated April 7, 2020. a) Require the MPDC to regularly prepare the required reports and post the said reports to at least 3 conspicuous public places; b) Send a written notice to DBM, House of Representatives, Senate of the Philippines, House Committee on Appropriations, Senate Committee on Finance after posting the required reports; and c) Strictly observe the timelines in the posting of the required operational reports at the designated places in order to attain maximum transparency in LGU governance.
observations.managementAction: Management addressed their reasons for the delays on the unimplemented projects in a formal reply dated March 8, 2022. observations.recommendation: a) Coordinate with the DSWD regularly to enhance or improve the distribution of succeeding subsidy programs; b) Increase their manpower to facilitate the distribution, accomplishment and encoding of SAC forms and the preparation and approval of the payroll; and c) Strictly follow the schedule set to ensure the timely and efficient distribution of cash assistance to the beneficiaries. a) The LGU shall report to the DSWD about the noted duplication of cash beneficiaries for further validation/investigation and appropriate action; b) The DSWD shall strictly intensify the validation process before and after the payout to avoid double claims or same scenario to happen in the future. observations.recommendation:
We recommended that:
a) The MPDC should always ensure that all the expenses incurred is included on the approved investment plan;
b) Prepare and submit additional/updated supplemental investment plan for the programs and projects intended to locally stranded individuals and returning overseas Filipino; and
c) Incorporate the updated investment plan to the supplemental budget and submit the said plans to COA.

We recommended and the Management agreed to:
a) Require the Local Finance Committee to strictly monitor the funds intended for the programs and project;
b) Require the respective office to implement PPAs according to the timeframes, approved budgets, and intended purposes; and
c) Return any unutilized balance to the National Treasury after the lifting of the State of Calamity in accordance to Section 3.9 of DBM LBC No. 125 dated April 7, 2020.

We recommended and the Management agreed to:
a) Require the MPDC to regularly prepare the required reports and post the said reports to at least 3 conspicuous public places;
b) Send a written notice to DBM, House of Representatives, Senate of the Philippines, House Committee on Appropriations, Senate Committee on Finance after posting the required reports; and
c) Strictly observe the timelines in the posting of the required operational reports at the designated places in order to attain maximum transparency in LGU governance.

We recommended that the LGU:
a) Coordinate with the DSWD regularly to enhance or improve the distribution of succeeding subsidy programs;
b) Increase their manpower to facilitate the distribution, accomplishment and encoding of SAC forms and the preparation and approval of the payroll; and
c) Strictly follow the schedule set to ensure the timely and efficient distribution of cash assistance to the beneficiaries.

We recommended the following courses of actions:
a) The LGU shall report to the DSWD about the noted duplication of cash beneficiaries for further validation/investigation and appropriate action;
b) The DSWD shall strictly intensify the validation process before and after the payout to avoid double claims or same scenario to happen in the future. Ensure that no payout shall be allowed, unless complete validation process that consists of eligibility and duplication checks are performed by them and by the LGU and other concerned agencies; and
c) Compel the duplicate beneficiaries to return the excess SAP benefits to the government; Disqualify them from receiving the next tranche of the cash assistance as emphasized in Item VIII B. (6.2) of DSWD Memo Circular No. 09 series of 2020.

We recommended and the Management agreed to:
a) Ascertain that provisions in the Republic Act No. 10121 are properly complied specifically the allotment for the Local DRRM Fund which will improve the capacity of the Municipality to have a well-defined and budgeted Local DRRM Plan;
b) Ensure that allotted budget for the Local DRRM Fund were properly appropriated for the year to ascertain that all programs, projects and activities identified in the Local DRRM Fund will be realized accordingly and reported appropriately; and
c) Properly implement the Local DRRM Plan and optimize the utilization of its Local DRRM Fund to lessen the socioeconomic and environmental impacts of disasters in the community and disaster risks, and to enhance disaster preparedness and response capabilities of the community.

We recommended and the Management agreed to:
a) Maximize the utilization of the 20% development fund allocated for the implementation of various projects, programs and activities that are envisioned to help in the attainment of social, economic and environmental development and optimally utilize the 20% development fund to help achieve desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes;
b) Appropriate 20% development fund exclusively to programs, projects and activities which are in the nature of investment or capital expenditures in accordance with the DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular 2017-01, unless there were other provisional guidelines that would supplement the laws, rules and regulations previously issued; and
c) Ascertain that programs, projects and activities should be specifically identified, quantified, monitored, accomplished and reported in accordance with plans and targets set for the year.

We recommended that the Agency Head should institutionalize an Inventory Committee to conduct actual physical inventory of its PPE annually, taking into consideration the required standards and procedures in Planning, Execution, and Reconciliation of GSO and Accounting records. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to maintain and update the PPE ledger cards and the GSO of its property cards as control records to facilitate the regular reconciliation of records and investigate any discrepancy noted. Require the concerned personnel to follow the guidelines in the preparation and submission of Property Inventory Form as prescribed in COA Circular 2018-002 to serve as basis of the GSIS and other concerned agencies in evaluating the general insurance coverage over all insurable assets and properties of the Municipalities. And ensure that all insurable physical assets are adequately and reliably protected against any damage to or loss of properties pursuant to Republic Act No. 656. Lastly, we recommend that Management facilitate the preparation of documentary requirements to secure the Transfer Certificates of Title (TCT) under the name of the Municipality of Matalam, Cotabato for all parcels of land to support claims of ownership and to avoid loss of properties in case other interested parties may claim the same in the future; and trace its records/documents to establish ownership of the parcels of land with incomplete data as to acquisition date and cost and if found to be legally owned by the Municipality, expedite the application for the land titles under the name of the Municipality of Matalam, Cotabato.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2019 The Accounting and MGSO Office is now in the process of reviewing, reconciling and updating the records of Property, Plant & Equipment (PPEs) ledger card and property cards. Management warranted that the Office of the MGSO is religiously complying on the renewal of the insurance schedules of heavy equipment and allocated an amount for the insurance of heavy equipment. Require the concerned units and personnel to follow the guidelines in the preparation and submission of the Property Inventory Form (PIF) as prescribed in COA Circular 2018-002 to be submitted to the GSIS Office as scheduled. The Municipal Assessor, together with the Municipal Treasurer and Inventory Committee will look into the documents (Deed of Sale, Deed of Donations, Homestead Transfer of Rights, Deed of Exchange) on hand and request funds from the Local Chief Executive for the transfer of titles in the name of the Municipal Government of Matalam.
observations.reason: The process of reviewing, reconciling and updating the records was still in progress due to the immensity of the account. Hence, this was reiterated and incorporated in Part II of this AAR.

the GSIS to have an adequate and reliable protection against any damage to
or loss of properties pursuant to R.A. No. 656;
b. Require the concerned personnel to follow the guidelines in the preparation
and submission of the PIF as prescribed in Section 5.1 of COA Circular
2018-002; and
c. Submit the required Property Inventory Form (PIF) on or before April 30 of
each year to serve as basis of the GSIS in evaluating the general insurance
coverage over all insurable assets and properties of the Municipality.

We recommended that Management ensure the implementation of the
planned activities and projects as incorporated in the LDRRMF Investment Plan
to increase the level of disaster preparedness and response capability of the
Local Government Unit.

We recommended that Management require the LDRRM Officer to
submit annually a comprehensive Local Disaster Risk Reduction and
Management Fund Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP) compliant to COA Circular
2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 containing a complete functional
classification, list of specific projects and activities with sufficient details of
funding whether current year appropriation, continuing appropriation or
special trust funds from unexpended DRRM Funds of prior years, copy furnish
the COA Audit Team. Likewise require the LDRRM Officer to submit monthly
the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRM and the Municipal Accountant
to add on the Notes to Financial Statements the details on the unexpended
balance of the said fund.

the provisions of Circular No. 2013-004 and ensure regular submission of the list
of projects at the beginning of the year in proper format as well as the required
quarterly information of PPAs of the municipality.
